1.intro.0.text: The Fifth Republic has run its course. A small elite, led by the president-monarch, is disorganizing the state and ruling for its own benefit. Macron is even trampling on election results, just as he did with the results of the French vote in the 2005 referendum on the European Constitution. This system pits the French against one another. It erases the people in favor of an ever-increasing authoritarian drift. Today's France no longer resembles the France of 1958, when the current Constitution was adopted. This new France must be matched by a new Republic. This will be the means for the people to rebuild themselves by writing the rules of the game: the new Constitution. The citizens' revolution aims to advance democracy and popular participation everywhere: in institutions, but also in cities and businesses. It seeks to establish the primacy of the general interest over the particular interests of the ultra-rich and the largest landowners in the country. Finally, France must reconnect with the love of freedom.
